div.head: The Minister in Portugal (Norweb) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
1755. In light of wolfram settlement and as it appears improbable that we shall be able to present supply purchase proposals immediately,
I believe it of considerable importance that we provide Portuguese authorities within next few days with some tangible evidence of our intention to give Portugal economic benefits in return for step which has been taken. It is highly desirable also that a favorable atmosphere should prevail during time when Portuguese will be formulating their wolfram control regulations. Accordingly, I should like to inform them that we propose to cancel all surcharges after June 30, if Department approves.
Urgent reply to this cable is requested.
British Embassy is sending a parallel cable to London.
Sent Department; to London as 273; to Madrid by courier.
